page: OVHcloud b3-512 is a general-purpose server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration delivers 128 dedicated physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. This CPU setup is paired with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 20 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores all placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory performance is also strong, with small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth reaching the top 10%, while large-block uncached read and write bandwidths rank in the top 25%. In application-specific tests, the server demonstrates top-tier capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. Redis SET operations also achieve top-tier throughput at 169.74 million operations per second.

Despite the high core count, certain single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression perform in the middle 50% tier. Additionally, while prompt processing for large language models up to 70B parameters ranks in the top 25%, text generation for a 70B model falls into the bottom 10% due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. This resource profile is highly suited for highly parallelized workloads, multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, offering high resource density and compute efficiency for non-GPU-bound enterprise applications.
description: OVHcloud b3-512 is a KVM-based general-purpose server featuring 128 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. It also achieves top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations. However, single-threaded gzip compression is average, and text generation for large 70B language models is poor due to the absence of a GPU. This configuration is optimized for highly parallelized workloads, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-512 is a KVM-based general-purpose server with 128 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 512 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-512 features 128 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 512 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated 128-core AMD EPYC server with 512 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance and high-speed NVMe storage.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with no hyperthreading.
512 GB of system memory paired with a 400 GB NVMe SSD.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and software compilation benchmarks.
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and 1 public IPv4 address.
Poor text generation performance for large language models due to zero GPU allocation.
Suited for multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and static web serving.
